VerbalDescription: Resize the grid to be a single grid unit. Fill the entire grid with the color of the shape in the original input that had blank spaces cut out.
Find the shape that is not solid and create a 1x1 grid that is the same color as that shape.
one of the blocks has an empty center. make a 1x1 grid and fill it with that blocks color
do a grid size 1x1 so it is just one grid square. colour block the 1 grid square green
Look at the original grid. Notice how it makes shapes. Look at the color of the hollow shape. Your job is to make a 1x1 grid the of that color.
Find the shape that has the holes in the middle, then make a 1x1 size grid and colour in the block the same colour as that shape. (In this case Green)
Look at the shape that is not solidly filled with a colour or has a black centre.  Change the grid height and width to one and colour this in the same colour as this shape.
Change the grid size to 1x1. The colour of this grid, must be the same colour as the object that has at least one black square within it's 'pattern'.
the color of the object with the missing central grid
A single color becomes the entire solution.
Create a single grid, color it with the color of the shape which has a hole.
Create a 1 x 1 grid. Fill this square with the color that matches the shape with a missing piece.
Make grid 1x1. use the colour of the shape which isn't completely filled in.
Identify a non-black shape that is not solid (contains at least one black square inside of it). Create a grid of 1x1. Use the colour of the shape you identified earlier.
Make the grid 1x1. Fill the block with the colour of the original shape which is hollow or has one or more squares of colour missing
the answer is just a 1 cell grid size and the colour of the cell is taken from the one object that is hollow
resize grid to 1x1 .Take whatever shape has a black cube in the middle and make the grid cube the color of that shape .
Fill in the shape with a checkerboard pattern

The grid should be resized from the input. If the pattern is fully filled in, it is complete. If the pattern within the shape is not completed, complete the pattern by filling in alternate squares using the same colors shown.

new grid should be same dimensions as old grid.
locate the design in the old grid.  the design should have a green perimeter and a black/yellow checker pattern.  
copy the green perimeter exactly on the new grid. 
if the black/yellow checker pattern does not completely fill the inside of the green perimeter, you must complete it also on the new grid.  
on the other hand, if the checker pattern is already complete, no further action is needed.  simply copy the shape as is.
copy the grid size from the original. Copy the pattern to fill up the inside of the green frame.
Use the same grid size as in the input. There will be one object in the input. In your answer, continue the checkered pattern of the object. (keep the color of the original pattern the same)
Copy the test input grid and continue the pattern of yellow cells inside the green shape by changing every other black cell to yellow until the green shape is full.
Keep grid size the same as the input. Copy object in the input exactly keeping all colors the same. Complete the unfinished checkerboard pattern from the input for the solution.

Copy the input so the the output grid matches this. here we are filling the space inside the green squares with alternating black and yellow squares. Move through the object to add the alternating squares until the whole green object is filled
The green color and finish in given box
Simply copy from input.  The rule is to fill in the checkerboard pattern that is missing from within the green border, if anything is missing.  Nothing is missing, so simply copy and you're done.

Match the grid size to the Test input. If the object copied over has a missing area of checkered pattern within it, complete the pattern, and if it is already complete, copy it over just the way it is.
Use the same grid size as in the input. Copy the object from the input. Fill in any squares with yellow to complete the pattern within the object.

Keep the same green board as the test shape.  Also, keep the same grid size.  Completely fill in the the green board shape with a yellow and black checkboard pattern that matches the test.  It is okay if both are exactly the same.
The grid and shape stay the same. The only exception is if the center pattern of yellow isn't filled in. Then it would be filled in but since it was already completed no changes are made.

Copy from the input grid. If there are any tiles within the outer border of the shape that do not have a checkerboard pattern, fill in those remaining black spaces with the same checkerboard pattern (and color) that fills the remainder of the inner space of the object.
Copy grid from input. Fill any blank space with the yellow and black checkerboard pattern. If no blank space exists within the object, do nothing!
copy the grid. complete the yellow and black checkerboard patter within the green perimeter
Use the same grid size as in the input. There will be one object in the input; in your answer, shift the object one grid square to the right.
resize the grid to be the same. copy the green exterior. fill the pattern of the interior to fill the entire interior the checkerboard pattern.

Copy the grid from input. Color all of the blocks inside the green frame so it alternates between yellow and black blocks. No two black blocks inside the green should be together other than diagonally. No two yellow blocks should be together other than diagonally. It should look like a checkerboard pattern.
The shape repeats itself.
there is a green shape filled with a yellow/balck check pattern, this pattern should be filled into the entire green shape but not any further, keeping the grid size and colours the same

Copy the input from the demonstration screen. Continue the checkerboard pattern of yellow blocks until it meets the green outline of the shape. If the checkerboard is already fully completed leave it as it is.

Resize the grid as seen in the input. Copy the shape. The shape should have a checkered pattern, if there are gaps within the pattern then fill it in, but if the pattern if complete then do not make any changes.
Change the grid size to that of the demonstration, and copy the image in the demonstration keeping it exactly as shown.
Look at the shape of the largest solid figure. Resize the grid to match that shape exactly. Do not include the figure, the grid should be empty of colored blocks.

Look at the shapes on the test input grid. Change your grid size to match the size and shape of whichever colour shape is filled one colour, meaning there is no black inside it. Your new grid should be black.
Identify the shape that is filled in (not hollow). Resize the answer grid to be the same dimensions as this shape but leave the grid completely black.
resize the grid to be the same dimensions as the one shape that is whole and doesn't have a hole in the middle. then make the whole grid blank
There are four objects in the original. Focus on the blue object, ignore all other objects. In your answer, the grid is the same size as the blue object, and the colour is changed to black.
Resize the grid so that it is the same shape and size as the filled in pattern on the original. Colour it all black.

grid size equals the size of the filled in rectangle

You will see solid and outlined shapes. Choose the solid shape. Make a grid the same number and orientation of squares as the solid shape. Leave the grid all black. That is the final answer.
Resize the box grid to the same dimension as the solid color item in the input box.
Find a collection of tiles that are completely filled inside and use a grid of that same size but leave it empty.
View grid on left. Copy dimensions of object that is colored within grid. Resize grid to match colored object inside grid. Use flood fill to recolor grid black.
Make a blank(black) grid of the shape that is filled(no black in the middle)
good correction
Find the shape that is solid, and then make the grid size the exact same as that shape. Leave it black.
Find the object that is solid without a hole in the middle. Change the grid size to match this object. Change the grid to contain all black boxes.
look at the size of the solid colour from the original. make the grid the same size as this shape. Leave this grid blank/black and submit.
Create a grid of a shape of one of the figures, fill with black. Try different figures if the first does not work.

You should see multiple shapes. Look for the shape that is filled in completely, rather than the other shapes that have a hole in the middle. You should resize the grid to the size and shape of the filled-in shape. Do NOT colour in the shape, leave the grid black.

Identify the solid shape within your group of shapes, e.g. all parts of the shape are the same colour. Then create a grid with the same dimensions as that specific shape and change the colour to all black
Inside box only select
Find the hollow center piece. Make that the entire grid. If it's a 1x2 green center, make a 1x2 green grid.
There are many shapes in the input. There will be one shape which is like a border. See what is in the centre of the border and make the whole grid those dimensions and that colour
Make the grid size 1 height and 2 width and colour the grid green.

There will be 3 shapes in the input. One of these will have a different colour inside of it. Your answer will be the different coloured section inside of the object. Make the grid the same size as the enclosed shape and keep it the same colour

Look at the shapes shown. There should be one shape that is not a solid colour, with different coloured blocks in the middle. Make the grid size the same size as this inner shape, and then flood fill it to be the same colour as this inner shape.

In the middle of 1 of the input objects will be a cell or cells of a different colour. Grid dimension is the same size as this different colour cell or cells and the colour is the same, so completed grid is an exact copy of the different colour cell/cells in the middle of one of the objects
FILLING INNER CELLS TO SELECTED.
Find the shape that has an outline and isn't just one solid color. Resize the grid to match the inside of that outlined shape, and fill the grid to match the color.
Find the object that's made of two color squares. Make the grid the size of the innermost shape and fill it that color, recreating that inner space with your grid.
The rule here is to recreate the shape within the hollow square of the input grid. Exactly the same colour/size.

Look at the shape that has a different colour inside of it (e.g green border, red inside). Then, take note of the dimensions of the colour inside the shape (e.g 5x2) and resize the grid to those dimensions. Completely fill the grid with the colour that's inside one of the shapes presented in the input (only one shape should have a different colour inside it).

Make the grid size match the hole or gap in the middle of one of the original shapes. Then make the whole grid match the colour of the shape, as if you are providing the missing piece to complete the original shape
Look for the colored square inside of another square. Extract it and make the grid size the same dimensions of the inner square. Fill the grid with the same color.
Find the open square. Count how many squares are inside of the open square. Make a grid that size. Fill the color to match what's inside the square.
Resize grid to match center of the feature. Copy the feature.

Look to see which pattern has a different interior color as its border. Now resize you grid to be the length and width of that interior and color the new grid the same color as the interior of the original pattern.

You will see a grid made up of one or more rectangular shapes. One of the shapes has a gap within that is filled to a different colour, forming another shape within. Focus on the shape that falls within, and set your grid height and width to the same size as this shape. Then set the entirety of your resized grid to the same colour as the shape.
There are two objects. Focus on the inner square (yellow). Use a grid size that is equal to the inner square. The colour remains unchanged--yellow.
Change the grid size to the size of the shape within the outline of the bigger shape (7x7). Then change the colour to the colour of that shape (yellow).

if the shape is just a outline(no fill) then fill it.  Else just leave it has it is

You have a yellow shape which may be solid yellow or may have black in the middle.. Fill it in with yellow so that it is solid. If it was solid to begin with then don't make any changes at all.
If the shape in the input is an outline only/hollow, use the exact same grid size and fill in the outline, to create a solid shape of the same colour

use the same size grid as the input. draw out the same shape in the input with the same colour but fill in any gaps in the shape so it is one solid shape with the corresponding colour.

Keep grid size and object colour/position the same as input. If the object is a solid colour do nothing, if the object has uncoloured squares inside its outline fill in the squares in the same colour as the object.

Use the copy from input button.
If the copied is "empty" contiains the black background colour, the use the flood fill option to fill the shape with the same colour as the shape outline.

If the shape is filled in, then copy it exactly as it is. If the shape has holes in it, copy the shape but fill in the holes with the same color.

if the shape is hollow then fill it in, other wise remove the fill and only leave the outline

The input has either a solid shape or an outline of a shape. The output grid should be the same size as the input, with the same shape in the same color. If the input shape is an outline, fill it to make it completely solid. If the input shape was filled, make its interior black, so that the shape is outlined with single-block lines.
Copy the grid. Invert the shape. If its filled, empty it. If its outlined, fill it.
fill the shape with the same color
Copy from input. If shape is not filled in, flood fill in the same colour. If shape is filled in, shade in squares black so you are just left with the outline.
Copy out the grid from the input. Colour the inside of the image with the same colour squares as its border.

Copy the shape and grid size from the input. If the shape is just an outline that is not filled with color on the inside, fill it in your solution with the same color. If it is filled, erase the color on the inside so you only end up with the outline of the same color.
The hollow shapes get filled in and the solid shapes get hollowed out, leaving only enough squares to retain the shape.

Copy the original grid and all its elements as is. Identify the objects that have uncolored cells within them. Fill in those uncolored cells with the color of the object surrounding them so that the object is now a fully colored, solid shape. Then identify the objects that were already fully colored in. Paint the inside of these shapes black so that you end up with all cells within that object being blacked out and only the outermost cells of the original shape remain in their color.

Copy the input grid and objects exactly. Now you will fill in the objects with the uncolored squares to create a solid colored object. Then hollow out the solid input objects by coloring the inside squares black and leaving just the outer layer.

take each shape if they have a space in the middle fill this in and if there is no space then make the shape have a space, one square thick.

The rule is to change any shapes in the original grid that are not filled in to be completely filled in with the same color as its' border and any shapes that are already filled in to be hollowed out with black squares so that they only have a border as their shape. In this case, you will fill in the light blue triangle on top with light blue squares and hollow out the green triangle on bottom with black squares so that it is only a green-bordered upside-down triangle.
Match the grid size. Copy the images over and invert them - The ones with a border - fill those. The filled ones, make them into a border

You will see some shapes, separated either left or right or top or bottom of the screen. Some of these shapes will be outlines, and some will be solid filled blocks of color.

Flip the shapes, so if they started solid filled shapes on the left and outlines on the right, they are now outlines on the left and filled shapes on the right. Do not change the colours

There will be a number of shapes, either coloured outlines, or filled colours. Recreate the shapes in the same grid size, except the shapes that were outlines should now be filled, and the filled shapes should not be outlines. The colours should remain the same.

Use the same grid size as the input. You should see various shapes, some with holes in and some without holes. In your solution/output, you should copy these shapes exactly (shape, position, colour, orientation, size), but you should fill in the ones will holes, and create holes in the ones that are filled. When creating the holes in the filled shapes, you should only leave a one-block wide outline from the outside of the shape.

Adjust the width and height of the grid to match the ones in the input. Copy the design of the input.

There should be several shapes in the input, and they can be classified into "not fully filled" (shapes that have a massive hole in the middle and are not fully filled with the same colour) and those that are "fully filled" (shapes that are fully filled with the same colour).

Identify which shapes belong into which categories, and fully fill shapes belong to the "not fully filled" category with the same colour. For shapes of the "fully filled" category, colour the "middle"/interior of these shapes with black.

You will either get a solid block, or two line filled. If you have two lines: copy the image over exactly (use the button) and fill with the same color to make a solid block. If you have a solid block, copy the image over, and black out all but the top and bottom lines, so that you have two lines.

The input contains parallel lines made from diagonally adjacent colored cells on the grid. Copy the input in the same size and color. Fill in all cells in the rectangle in the same color as the diagonal lines.

connect the two isolated strings by drawing in two sides connecting them, then fill in the middle of these borders with the same color. The result should be a filled in rectangle.

Keep the color, grid, and position of the object the same. Delete all the squares inside the longest edges of the object (for example, a rectangle would only have the two longest edges left after removing all the interior squares).

You are either presented with a shape or two lines. The grid should be 11 vertical x 14 horizontal. If presented with two lines, you need to fill the space between them in the original colour creating a shape with no gaps. You can do this by joining the two lines together to create a quadrilateral, and then using the fill function. Conversely, if you begin with a shape, imagine you are only keeping the shape's long edges, same colour and same position, leaving you with two lines.
